commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer proof handling for a visible Telegram transport path, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff changes TypeScript rendering/display logic and tests only; it adds no dependency, workflow, package, credential, or supply-chain surface. (sha=8451f06049b1e81141461347779ca33710c92f82)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Telegram progress draft rendering and draft-stream transport so progress preview text is plain by default, retains rich HTML for rich previews, maps API progress items to API display metadata, and updates focused tests.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +130. Total +153 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.9 still return HTML parse-mode Telegram progress draft previews, and the linked issue points to existing Telegram progress tests that assert the noisy HTML/code payloads.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer proof handling for a visible Telegram transport path, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): render progress draft rows as plain readable text
target.body:
## Summary Fixes #95002. Telegram progress draft previews now keep their canonical preview text plain and readable instead of making ordinary tool/progress rows depend on Telegram HTML/code wrappers. Before this change, progress drafts asserted payloads like: ```html Shelling
🛠️ Exec command false exit 2 ``` After this change, the same draft is represented as readable plain text: ```text Shelling 🛠️ exit 2; command false ``` ## Problem The Telegram progress draft renderer used ``, ``, ``, and `
` for transient progress rows. This made command/tool progress noisy even when HTML parsed successfully, and any fallback/sanitized rendering could expose transport markup to the user instead of a useful status line. ## Changes - Stop wrapping Telegram progress draft lines in Markdown backticks/code spans. - Normalize Telegram progress draft rows to plain readable text first. - Mark progress draft previews for plain default transport so non-rich Telegram drafts send `TelegramDraftPreview.text` directly instead of first sending attached `richMessage.html` with `parse_mode: "HTML"`. - Preserve rich-message support by continuing to build escaped HTML from the same normalized plain line model when rich messages are explicitly enabled. - Cover `api` progress edge cases by mapping `itemKind: "api"` to the API tool display and adding a first-class `API` display config. - Update Telegram dispatch and draft-stream tests to assert readable progress draft text, plain non-rich transport, and API progress rendering. ## Verification Local targeted checks after the API edge follow-up: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts \ telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \ telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts \ telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/streaming.test.ts src/agents/tool-display.test.ts git diff --check ``` Results: - `telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`: 132 passed - `telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts`: 48 passed - `telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts`: 41 passed - `src/channels/streaming.test.ts`: 4 passed - `src/agents/tool-display.test.ts`: 41 passed - `git diff --check`: passed ## Risk Low-to-moderate Telegram presentation risk: - This intentionally changes Telegram progress draft preview text from HTML-formatted rows to plain readable rows. - Final answer rendering is not changed. - Normal Telegram markdown/HTML answer rendering remains outside this progress-draft path. - Rich progress previews still get escaped HTML generated from the normalized plain line model. ## Not addressed This PR does not change Telegram draft materialization policy, including first-preview debounce/minimum length or progress-mode answer-lane suppression. That separate behavior is tracked in #95004 because it involves a product tradeoff between notification noise and Web preview parity. ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram progress draft preview rows no longer expose HTML/code-style transport formatting for ordinary tool progress. The after-fix preview text is readable plain status text. - **Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw checkout on this PR head. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the patched Telegram progress-preview path locally and captured the resulting preview payload text for the issue examples. - **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the patched local checkout: ```text after-fix Telegram progress preview payload: Shelling 🛠️ exit 2; command false After-fix non-command progress preview payload: Shelling 🔎 Web Search: docs lookup • tests passed ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The preview payload contains readable progress lines and no ``, ``, ``, or `
` transport markup in the non-rich Telegram draft text path. - **What was not tested:** Live Bot API delivery in a public chat was not exercised for this formatting-only patch; final answer rendering is outside the changed progress-draft path. ## Native Telegram Bot API proof A dedicated Telegram test bot was used for native Bot API proof. Sensitive values are intentionally not included: the bot token is omitted and the private test chat id is redacted. - Dedicated test bot: `@snowzlmOpenClawtestBot` (`8602965171`) - Chat: redacted private test chat - Token: not included - Generated at: `2026-06-20T06:22:49.182Z` Scenarios verified: 1. PR #95007 — progress draft rows are visible as readable Telegram text. - Operation: `sendMessage` + `editMessageText` with Telegram HTML parse mode. - Message id: `4` - Before: `Shelling` - After: `Shelling` plus compact `exec running ...` and `exec passed ...` rows. - Checks: Bot API accepted the HTML payload; no `[object Object]` or raw object dump; rows are readable as plain text in the Telegram message. 2. PR #95183 — short first preview is bounded, not indefinitely silent. - Operation: delayed `sendMessage` after an intentional initial quiet window. - Message id: `5` - `minInitialChars`: `30` - Proof delay: `1201ms` - Text: `Short` - Checks: initially quiet; then materialized despite being below threshold. 3. PR #95183 — progress mode can show a stable label placeholder before final delivery. - Operation: placeholder `sendMessage`, followed by normal final `sendMessage`. - Placeholder message id: `6` - Final message id: `7` - Checks: placeholder visible as `Shelling`; final delivery is separate and normal; answer text is not leaked into the placeholder. Local sanitized artifact retained in the maintenance workspace: `projects/openclaw-pr-maintenance/proofs/telegram-real/telegram-proof-1781936573613.public.md`. ### Telegram draft lifecycle proof Additional native Bot API lifecycle proof was captured to address draft-message count and cleanup behavior: - Dedicated test bot: `@snowzlmOpenClawtestBot` (`8602965171`) - Chat/token: redacted - Local sanitized artifact: `projects/openclaw-pr-maintenance/proofs/telegram-real/telegram-proof-draft-lifecycle-1781937832925.public.md` Verified scenarios: 1. Progress draft lifecycle, covering #95007 and #95183: - Operations: `sendMessage(draft)` → `editMessageText(same draft id)` → `editMessageText(same draft id)` → `sendMessage(final)` → `deleteMessage(draft)`. - Checks: both draft edits reused the initial draft message id; the final answer was delivered as a separate normal message; `deleteMessage` for the draft returned success. 2. Short-preview lifecycle, covering #95183: - Operations: `sendMessage(short preview)` → `editMessageText(same id update)` → `editMessageText(same id final)`. - Checks: the short preview, later update, and final text all used the same Telegram message id, so the bounded short preview does not create multiple default draft messages.
target.author: snowzlmbot

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
